title: Persea grandibracteata
taxonomy: Persea grandibracteata is classified as a species of evergreen tree in the family Lauraceae. This family consists of approximately 90 genera and more than 2,000 species, many of which are native to tropical and subtropical regions around the world. Members of the Lauraceae family are often known as laurel or laurels. The genus Persea is native to the Americas and includes several species of trees and shrubs. Persea grandibracteata is a unique species within this genus, characterized by its distinctive large bracts.

intro: Persea grandibracteata, also known as the large-bracted avocado plant, is a species of flowering plant in the laurel family, Lauraceae. The plant has been traditionally used in various folk medicines for its purported health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help in the treatment of wounds, fever, and other health issues. The bioactive constituents of Persea grandibracteata include alkaloids, flavonoids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Persea grandibracteata include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat a range of health conditions, including respiratory issues and gastrointestinal problems.
